title: CMDB Meta Envelope v1
description:
Every CMDB file written by a sensory tool MUST include this envelope. The meta block enables the Brain to detect schema versions, compute confidence from age, and identify the producing tool.

VENDORED COPY (V5-MOD-2 Phase 0, 2026-05-02). The canonical source of truth is in the LSP-Brains repository at `D:/Brains/LSP-Brains/schemas/cmdb-envelope-v1.schema.json` (or wherever LSP-Brains is checked out alongside NeuroGrim). The local copy here lets `neurogrim_core::sensor_conformance` validate sensor output without a path-dep on the spec repo. Drift between this copy and the canonical schema is detected by `cargo xtask schema-drift-check`. When the spec ships a v2 schema, mirror the change here + bump the file name (cmdb-envelope-v2.schema.json) + update the conformance suite's loader.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this snapshot was taken.

updated_by.description: Identifier of the tool that wrote this CMDB (e.g., 'check-test-health', 'jira-sensory').

source.description: Human-readable description of the external source observed.

score.description: Health score for this domain (0 = worst, 100 = best).

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp. Duplicates meta.updated_at for flat access by the scoring engine.

findings.description: Array of observation findings. Structure is domain-specific.

confidence.description: Optional sensor-supplied confidence in [0,100]. When absent, the Brain computes confidence by age-decay from meta.updated_at (spec §4.4 exponential-decay model). When present, the envelope value takes precedence — sensors with their own freshness signal (e.g., cache-age, registry-fetch staleness) MAY emit this directly. Added in spec v2.7 (Brains-2.0 E-B2-1).
